evidence: WordSketchApiServer.java (1253 lines) mixes HTTP server setup, route registration, query-parameter parsing, business-logic delegation, and JSON serialization in a single class. Handler methods like handleFullSketch and handleFullDependencySketch are structurally parallel but not abstracted.

The 6-line JSON serialization block for WordSketchResult (JSONObject word + put lemma/frequency/logDice/pos + collocations.add) is copy-pasted verbatim at lines 319-324, 364-369, 418-423, and 469-474 in the same file.

The relation-id normalization switch (adj_modifier->noun_modifiers, adj_predicate->noun_adj_predicates, subject_of->noun_verbs, object_of->verb_nouns) appears identically at lines 502-507 and 654-659 inside handleSemanticFieldExplore and handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti.

The QueryExecutor interface abstraction is systematically broken: executor is declared as QueryExecutor but is explicitly cast to BlackLabQueryExecutor five times (lines 306, 350, 403, 455, 694) to call executeSurfacePattern(), which is absent from the interface.

SemanticFieldExplorer.java (889 lines): explore() and exploreByPattern() implement the same 3-step algorithm (find collocates -> expand to candidates -> score by shared count) with ~130 lines of duplicated logic. exploreByPattern() even prints 'Step 3' twice (lines 179-180 and 206-207), a copy-paste residue. exploreByPredicates() is a trivial 2-line delegate to explore().

CollocationsBuilder.buildRelationCollocations() (~200 lines) sets simpleRelations = Collections.emptyList() immediately (line 226) and logs 'Relation precomputation is DISABLED', yet the full parallel processing infrastructure (ExecutorService, futures, batches, buildRelationEntriesForLemma calls) runs unconditionally and does nothing.

GrammarConfigLoader.deriveHeadPositionFromPattern() and deriveCollocatePositionFromPattern() (lines 209 and 251) are structurally identical ~40-line methods differing only in the label string searched ('1:' vs '2:'). They could be a single parameterized method.

Smaller utility classes (LemmaIdsCodec, StatisticsConverter, LemmaPredicate, SentenceDocument) are focused and clean. HybridIndexer has a coherent single responsibility (indexing sentences). CollocationsBuilder's build() method is complex but cohesive.

strengths: Small utility classes (LemmaIdsCodec, StatisticsConverter, LemmaPredicate) have sharply focused responsibilities—each does exactly one thing.
HybridIndexer cleanly separates indexing concerns (document creation, statistics collection, lemma ID assignment) with well-named helper objects.
GrammarConfigLoader validates its inputs rigorously on construction, making its contract explicit and its callers simple.
CollocationsBuilder.build() is a complex but coherent method—its responsibility (build the precomputed collocations file) is singular and the streaming/resume logic is self-contained.
design_coherence.issue_character: Issues cluster in the largest files as accumulated responsibilities: the API server is a true god class, and the two analysis-heavy classes (SemanticFieldExplorer, CollocationsBuilder) carry significant dead or duplicated algorithm bodies that should have been extracted.
design_coherence.score_rationale: The codebase scores below the 'solid but uneven' anchor (80) because the most important production files—the API surface and the core query algorithm—have clear structural problems: a god-class API server with repeated serialization and normalization blocks, a broken interface abstraction requiring repeated unsafe downcasts, and an 889-line explorer class with two parallel copies of the same 3-step algorithm. These are not edge-case files; they are the core execution paths. On the other hand, many peripheral classes are clean and focused, preventing this from falling to the 'significant drag everywhere' range (40-50). The score of 58 reflects: design competence in the small, but real structural drag concentrated where it matters most.

issues.summary: WordSketchApiServer (1253 lines) mixes HTTP wiring, routing, parameter parsing, JSON serialization, and business logic in one class
evidence: start() registers all routes inline with anonymous lambdas containing non-trivial logic
handleFullSketch, handleRelationQuery, handleFullDependencySketch, handleDependencyRelationQuery are ~50-80 line methods each combining parameter validation, query execution, and JSON assembly
The 6-line WordSketchResult-to-JSONObject serialization block is duplicated verbatim at lines 319-324, 364-369, 418-423, and 469-474
issues.suggestion: Extract a WordSketchResultSerializer helper that converts List<WordSketchResult> to JSONArray. Separate route dispatch (a thin routing class) from handler implementations (one class per handler group, e.g. SketchHandlers, SemanticFieldHandlers). The server class should only wire up the HTTP server and delegate.

issues.summary: QueryExecutor interface is bypassed by 5 explicit BlackLabQueryExecutor downcasts inside the API server
evidence: executor field is declared QueryExecutor but cast to BlackLabQueryExecutor at lines 306, 350, 403, 455, and 694 to call executeSurfacePattern()
executeSurfacePattern() is absent from the QueryExecutor interface, so the abstraction only holds at construction time
SemanticFieldExplorer also contains instanceof BlackLabQueryExecutor guards for the same reason (lines ~120-130)
issues.suggestion: Add executeSurfacePattern() to the QueryExecutor interface (or a richer sub-interface). Alternatively, if only BlackLabQueryExecutor will ever be used, remove the interface indirection and type the field directly; the polymorphism is fictional.

issues.summary: SemanticFieldExplorer.explore() and exploreByPattern() duplicate the same 3-step semantic field algorithm with ~130 lines of copied logic
evidence: Both methods execute: (1) find collocates for seed, (2) for each collocate find reverse nouns, (3) score nouns by shared-collocate count and logDice, (4) find core collocates
exploreByPattern() prints 'Step 3' twice (lines 179-180 and 206-207), exposing a copy-paste residue
exploreByPredicates() at line 262 is a 2-line delegate that adds no behavior
issues.suggestion: Extract a private exploreFromCollocates(seed, seedCollocScores, ...) that runs steps 2-4 given an already-fetched collocate map. Both explore() and exploreByPattern() differ only in how they fetch step-1 collocates; the rest is identical and should be shared.

issues.summary: CollocationsBuilder.buildRelationCollocations() has ~200 lines of dead code after explicitly disabling itself with Collections.emptyList()
evidence: Line 226: 'simpleRelations = Collections.emptyList()' with log message 'Relation precomputation is DISABLED'
Lines 230-303 iterate over simpleRelations (always empty), execute full parallel ExecutorService/Future infrastructure, and call buildRelationEntriesForLemma—all doing nothing
buildRelationEntriesForLemma() (~60 lines) is only called from this dead path
issues.suggestion: Either delete the method body entirely (keeping only an early-return stub) or, if future re-enabling is intended, remove the live infrastructure and replace with a single TODO comment. Dead code maintained alongside live code signals intent confusion and misleads reviewers.

issues.summary: GrammarConfigLoader has two near-identical ~40-line methods deriveHeadPositionFromPattern() and deriveCollocatePositionFromPattern() differing only in the label they seek
evidence: deriveHeadPositionFromPattern (line 209) and deriveCollocatePositionFromPattern (line 251) share the same tokenizer loop, skipping logic, and position counter
The only functional difference: head method returns on encountering '1:', collocate method returns on encountering '2:'
Both default to 1 and 2 respectively—same structure, different constant
issues.suggestion: Replace both with a single private static int derivePositionFromPattern(String pattern, int labelNumber) that accepts the label to search for. Call sites become derivePositionFromPattern(pattern, 1) and derivePositionFromPattern(pattern, 2).

issues.summary: The relation-id legacy-name normalization switch is copy-pasted identically into two adjacent handler methods in WordSketchApiServer
evidence: The 4-case switch (adj_modifier->noun_modifiers, adj_predicate->noun_adj_predicates, subject_of->noun_verbs, object_of->verb_nouns) appears at lines 502-507 in handleSemanticFieldExplore and at lines 654-659 in handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti
Any future legacy-name addition or change requires updating both sites
issues.suggestion: Extract a private normalizeRelationId(String id) method and call it from both handlers.

issues.summary: HybridConllUProcessor and tagging/ConllUProcessor share ~50 lines of near-identical CoNLL-U file parsing logic
evidence: Both contain the same UTF-8 lenient decoder setup, blank-line sentence boundary detection, '# text =' comment extraction, and token accumulation loop
Mechanical scan confirmed 51-line boilerplate block shared between the two files
The sentence-building logic (skip '-' tokens, extract word/lemma/upos/xpos/deprel, compute offsets) is similarly replicated
issues.suggestion: Extract a shared CoNLLUReader or CoNLLUIterator utility (e.g., in a parsing subpackage) that yields SentenceTokens objects. Both processors consume the iterator and delegate to their respective indexers. This also makes testing the parser independently straightforward.

root_causes: The API server grew by accretion—each new endpoint was added inline rather than routed to a separate handler class, causing the god-class pattern.
The QueryExecutor interface was defined too narrowly early on and never updated when BlackLab-specific methods were added, leading to pervasive unsafe downcasts.

SemanticFieldExplorer's two exploration methods appear to have evolved from a single method by copy-and-modify rather than refactoring to a shared core, leaving duplicate algorithm bodies and even a doubled println.
likely_symptoms: relation_normalization_switch_duplication is a symptom of api_server_god_class
dead_buildrelationcollocations_body may be a symptom of an incomplete migration from window-based to CQL-based collocations
